title: lolium multiflorum var. italicum
intro: Lolium multiflorum var. italicum, also known as Ryegrass, is a perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, Ryegrass has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be beneficial in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and stems are used in herbal preparations, often combined with other herbs, to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory problems. Ryegrass is also valued for its ability to promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and support the overall health and well-being of the body.
